title: Stellar Wave 1 — Stellar Testnet connectivity
goal: Stand up Stellar Testnet connectivity as a parallel 'body' for Compass, analogous to the existing Solana providers: a fail-safe testnet-only network config module, a Horizon + Soroban connection module, and a Friendbot funding helper. Pure connectivity/infra with no policy logic. The brain stays untouched and Solana keeps working unchanged in parallel.

context.precedent: back/services/solana/providers/ (solanaNetworkConfig.ts devnet-only guard, solanaConnection.ts singleton client construction)

openQuestions.question: Horizon vs Soroban RPC — which is primary for account-state reads in the co-signer flow? Classic Stellar multisig (signers + thresholds) lives in Horizon; Soroban is for smart-contract calls.
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Horizon is primary for classic multisig account-state reads (balances, signers, thresholds). Soroban RPC is constructed alongside it but reserved for smart-contract calls in later waves. stellarConnection exposes both clients.

openQuestions.question: Should Friendbot funding be a test-only helper or also a demo script entrypoint?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Implement fundTestnetAccount as a shared helper in friendbot.ts, usable by both backend tests and the Wave 6 demo script. No separate test-only copy.

acceptanceCriteria: stellarNetworkConfig.ts validates all five STELLAR_* vars: STELLAR_NETWORK, STELLAR_NETWORK_PASSPHRASE, STELLAR_HORIZON_URL, STELLAR_RPC_URL, STELLAR_FRIENDBOT_URL.
The config module accepts only STELLAR_NETWORK=testnet with the testnet passphrase 'Test SDF Network ; September 2015' and rejects any mainnet passphrase with a coded error.
The testnet-only guard mirrors the Solana devnet-only guard in solanaNetworkConfig.ts and never silently defaults to mainnet.
stellarConnection.ts exposes a Horizon Server client and a Soroban RPC client constructed from validated config.
friendbot.ts funds a fresh testnet account and the account subsequently reports a positive XLM balance.
.env.example documents the five STELLAR_* vars verbatim next to the SOLANA_* block.
The brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS contract, MCP proxy) is untouched and Solana behavior is unchanged.
nonGoals: No policy logic, scoring, or decisioning in this wave.
No changes to the policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS contract, or MCP proxy.
No changes to any Solana provider or Solana behavior.
No Soroban contract invocation or transaction submission.
No mainnet support of any kind.

tasks.title: Add @stellar/stellar-sdk dependency
tasks.description: Add @stellar/stellar-sdk to package.json dependencies so Horizon and Soroban RPC clients can be constructed. No source code changes beyond the manifest in this task.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for stellarNetworkConfig
tasks.description: Write failing tests for the testnet-only config module before implementation, covering all five STELLAR_* vars and the mainnet-rejection fail-safe.
testsSpec: getStellarNetworkConfig accepts STELLAR_NETWORK=testnet plus the exact testnet passphrase and returns all five fields
getStellarNetworkConfig throws unsupported_network for any non-testnet network value
getStellarNetworkConfig throws mainnet_forbidden when STELLAR_NETWORK_PASSPHRASE is a mainnet/public-network passphrase
getStellarNetworkConfig throws missing_network_config when a required STELLAR_* var is absent
getStellarNetworkConfig throws invalid_network_config when a STELLAR_* var is malformed
isSupportedStellarNetwork returns false for non-testnet input and never defaults to mainnet
No legacy/ import appears in the new config file

tasks.title: Implement stellarNetworkConfig testnet-only guard
tasks.description: Implement stellarNetworkConfig.ts mirroring solanaNetworkConfig.ts: coded error factory, resolve guard, and config builder reading the five STELLAR_* vars. Reject any mainnet passphrase; accept only testnet. Never default to mainnet.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for stellarConnection
tasks.description: Write failing tests asserting that the connection module builds Horizon and Soroban clients from the validated config.
testsSpec: getHorizonServer returns a Horizon Server client built from STELLAR_HORIZON_URL
getSorobanRpc returns a Soroban RPC client built from STELLAR_RPC_URL
Clients are constructed only from a validated StellarNetworkConfig (no hardcoded mainnet endpoints)
Repeated calls return the same singleton client instance
No legacy/ import appears in the new connection file

tasks.title: Implement stellarConnection Horizon + Soroban clients
tasks.description: Implement stellarConnection.ts mirroring solanaConnection.ts: lazy singleton getHorizonServer() and getSorobanRpc() constructed from the validated config. Horizon is primary for classic multisig account-state reads; Soroban RPC reserved for later smart-contract calls.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the Friendbot helper
tasks.description: Write failing tests for fundTestnetAccount before implementation, covering successful funding and error surfacing.
testsSpec: fundTestnetAccount funds a fresh testnet account via STELLAR_FRIENDBOT_URL
After funding, the account read through Horizon reports a positive XLM balance
fundTestnetAccount surfaces a clear error when Friendbot funding fails and does not swallow it
No legacy/ import appears in the new friendbot file

tasks.title: Implement the Friendbot funding helper
tasks.description: Implement friendbot.ts with fundTestnetAccount(publicKey), a shared helper usable by both backend tests and the Wave 6 demo script. Reads STELLAR_FRIENDBOT_URL from validated config.

tasks.title: Document STELLAR_* vars in .env.example
tasks.description: Add the five STELLAR_* variables verbatim to .env.example next to the existing SOLANA_* block: STELLAR_NETWORK=testnet, STELLAR_NETWORK_PASSPHRASE="Test SDF Network ; September 2015", STELLAR_HORIZON_URL=https://horizon-testnet.stellar.org, STELLAR_RPC_URL=https://soroban-testnet.stellar.org, STELLAR_FRIENDBOT_URL=https://friendbot.stellar.org. Edit only; no other lines changed.

tasks.title: Validate Stellar Wave 1 and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite and confirm Solana tests stay green, the brain is untouched, and no legacy/ imports were added. Stop for parent review.
testsSpec: npm run test:back passes including unchanged Solana provider tests
npm run lint exits clean (allowing the pre-existing app/layout.tsx Fast Refresh warning)
npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false exits clean
No file under back/services/{domains,mcp,support} (the brain) was modified
No legacy/ import was introduced
